title: How to make a paper airplane
description: Today I’ll teach you how to make a paper airplane in under two minutes

steps.instruction: Take the left side of the paper and fold it into the right side, creating a crease in the middle.
steps.technique_notes: Align the left edge of the paper precisely with the right edge before pressing down to create a sharp, central crease.
steps.context: A single sheet of lined paper is laid flat on a wooden desk.
steps.success_criteria: The paper is folded perfectly in half lengthwise, with the left edge meeting the right edge, and a clear, sharp crease running down the center.
steps.common_failure_points: Uneven fold, not aligning edges properly, resulting in a crooked crease.
steps.failure_triggers: Misaligned paper edges, a wavy or indistinct central crease.

steps.instruction: Unfold the paper to reveal the central guideline.
steps.technique_notes: Gently unfold the paper to avoid tearing or creating additional, unwanted creases.
steps.context: The paper is folded in half lengthwise with a central crease.
steps.success_criteria: The paper is flat again, with a single, clear, and visible crease running down its center, serving as a guideline.
steps.common_failure_points: Ripping the paper, creating additional creases, or not fully flattening the paper.
steps.failure_triggers: Paper tearing, multiple creases visible, paper not lying flat.

steps.instruction: Fold the top-left and top-right corners of the paper inwards, aligning their inner edges with the central crease to form a triangle at the top.
steps.technique_notes: Fold each top corner so that its inner edge meets the central crease. Ensure the top point of the resulting triangle is sharp and symmetrical.
steps.context: The paper is unfolded with a central crease.
steps.success_criteria: Two triangular folds are made at the top of the paper, meeting perfectly along the central crease, forming a larger, symmetrical triangle shape with a sharp apex.
steps.common_failure_points: Corners not meeting precisely at the center, uneven triangles, or not creating a sharp point.
steps.failure_triggers: A gap between the folded edges at the center, an uneven or blunt top point.

steps.instruction: Fold the entire triangular section downwards, aligning the base of the triangle with the horizontal line at the bottom of the folded corners.
steps.technique_notes: Ensure the new horizontal crease is straight and aligns perfectly with the bottom edge of the previously folded triangles.
steps.context: The paper has two top corners folded into a triangle, with a visible horizontal line at the base of this triangle.
steps.success_criteria: The top triangular section is folded down, creating a new horizontal crease that aligns precisely with the bottom edge of the initial triangular folds.
steps.common_failure_points: Not aligning the fold with the correct line, resulting in an uneven or crooked fold.
steps.failure_triggers: The fold line is visibly above or below the intended horizontal line, or the fold is not straight.

steps.instruction: Take the new top corners of the paper and fold them inwards diagonally, aligning them with the central crease. Ensure both sides are folded equally.
steps.technique_notes: Fold each new top corner towards the central crease, creating diagonal lines. Strive for perfect symmetry on both sides.
steps.context: The paper has the first triangle folded down, creating a new top edge with two corners.
steps.success_criteria: Two new diagonal folds are made, meeting precisely at the central crease, forming a smaller, inverted triangle shape at the top. Both folds are symmetrical.
steps.common_failure_points: Uneven diagonal folds, not meeting at the center, or lack of symmetry between the two sides.
steps.failure_triggers: Gaps or overlaps at the central crease, one side folded noticeably differently than the other.

steps.instruction: Fold the small triangular flap at the very bottom upwards and into itself to secure the previous folds.
steps.technique_notes: Tuck the small triangular flap in firmly to ensure all preceding folds are held securely in place.
steps.context: The paper has the diagonal folds completed, leaving a small triangular flap at the bottom.
steps.success_criteria: The small bottom triangular flap is folded upwards and secured, effectively locking the previous folds in place.
steps.common_failure_points: Not folding the flap completely, or not securing it firmly enough, causing previous folds to loosen.
steps.failure_triggers: The flap is loose, or previous folds are visibly coming undone.

steps.instruction: Fold the entire paper in half along the original central crease, with the folded sections on the outside.
steps.technique_notes: Ensure all layers of paper are aligned perfectly before pressing down to create a sharp, symmetrical fold.
steps.context: The paper has all previous folds completed and secured, forming a somewhat symmetrical shape.
steps.success_criteria: The paper is folded in half along its length, creating a symmetrical, narrower shape with all layers aligned.
steps.common_failure_points: Not folding exactly along the central crease, or uneven alignment of the multiple layers.
steps.failure_triggers: The paper appears crooked when folded, or the edges do not match up precisely.

steps.instruction: Fold down each top side of the folded paper to create the wings for your airplane. Ensure both wings are symmetrical.
steps.technique_notes: Create sharp, even creases for both wings. The angle of the fold will determine the wing shape and flight characteristics.
steps.context: The paper is folded in half lengthwise, forming a central body for the airplane.
steps.success_criteria: Two symmetrical wings are formed by folding down the top layers on both sides, creating a classic paper airplane shape with sharp, even creases.
steps.common_failure_points: Uneven wings, not folding sharply, or wings that are too thick or thin.
steps.failure_triggers: One wing is visibly larger or shaped differently than the other, or the wings are not straight.

steps.instruction: Hold the completed paper airplane from the bottom, ready for flight.
steps.technique_notes: Grasp the paper airplane by its underside, near the center of gravity, to ensure a stable throw.
steps.context: The paper airplane is fully assembled with wings and all folds secured.
steps.success_criteria: The paper airplane is held correctly, ready to be thrown, with all folds secure and symmetrical, demonstrating a complete paper airplane.
steps.common_failure_points: Holding the plane incorrectly, which might cause folds to loosen or affect flight.
steps.failure_triggers: The plane appears flimsy, or folds are visibly coming apart when held.
